message:
chore: add rch per-agent remote build directory pattern to .gitignore and update bead issue tracker

- Add .rch-target-*/ glob to .gitignore to exclude per-agent remote
  build directories created by rch (remote compilation helper), which
  are ephemeral build artifacts that should never be committed
- Update bead issue tracker with latest status changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3odgj","title":"Make replay schema mismatches compare cleanly instead of failing validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:04:12.998920950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:59.707042506Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:59.707019753Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3odv","title":"Epic: Differential Extension Conformance Testing (TS-Rust Oracle)","description":"# Epic: Differential Extension Conformance Testing (TS-Rust Oracle)

## The Core Principle

Every extension that works in pi-mono TypeScript runtime MUST produce IDENTICAL behavior in our Rust QuickJS runtime, with ZERO source code modifications. This is not a theoretical goal -- it is a hard requirement for production readiness.

## Why Differential Testing (Not Fixture Replay)

Previous approach: capture TS outputs once, save as fixtures, replay in Rust.
Problem: fixtures are stale, hard to maintain, don't catch edge cases.

New approach: Run the SAME unmodified extension code through BOTH runtimes simultaneously, with the SAME inputs/mocks, and compare outputs directly. The TypeScript pi-mono runtime IS the oracle. This is differential/oracle testing.

Benefits:
- No fixture staleness -- always testing against live TS behavior
- Catches subtle differences in event ordering, error messages, type coercion
- Can test with ANY extension, not just ones we have written fixtures for
- Easy to add new extensions to the corpus -- just drop them in

## Architecture

Extension Source (.ts file) -> BOTH pi-mono (TS via Bun/jiti) AND Rust (swc+QuickJS) -> Compare outputs (registrations, event responses, hostcall results) -> PASS or FAIL

## Corpus (collected)

1,167+ TypeScript files across 5 tiers:
- 60 official pi-mono example extensions
- 58 community extensions
- 63 npm registry extensions
- 23 third-party GitHub extensions
- 7 agent collection extensions

## Key Technical Details

- pi-mono location: legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/
- Extension loader (TS): src/core/extensions/loader.ts -- uses jiti for TS loading
- Extension types (TS): src/core/extensions/types.ts -- ExtensionAPI, Extension, events
- Extension runner (TS): src/core/extensions/runner.ts -- lifecycle management
- Rust runtime: src/extensions.rs + src/extensions_js.rs -- QuickJS-based
- Bun 1.3.8: Available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun
- Artifacts: tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/

## Success Criteria

- 100% of official pi-mono extensions (60) produce identical output in both runtimes
- 90%+ of community extensions produce identical output
- Performance within 2x of TypeScript for load time, 1.5x for event dispatch
- Zero crashes, zero panics, zero undefined behavior
- CI pipeline runs full suite on every PR
- Machine-readable conformance report generated per run","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:13:53.326652907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:33.743363158Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:23.921140367Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3485,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SUPERSEDES EXISTING BEADS: This epic replaces the previous fragmented conformance testing beads (bd-6vcm, bd-2jlj, bd-c6xq, bd-2bis) with a unified DIFFERENTIAL TESTING approach. Key change: instead of capturing TS fixtures once and replaying in Rust, we run BOTH runtimes on every test and compare directly. The TS pi-mono runtime IS the oracle.

RELATED EXISTING BEADS: bd-6vcm (Feature: Extension Conformance Testing), bd-2jlj (Epic: Full Pi Extension Conformance), bd-c6xq (Reference capture harness), bd-2bis (Fixture-based conformance tests), bd-382l (Phase 1: Research & Source Acquisition), bd-2vrw (Phase 2: Test Infrastructure). These should be considered superseded by this epic's phases.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:41Z"},{"id":3486,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORPUS INVENTORY: As of 2026-02-05, we have 1,167 TypeScript files across 5 tiers in tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/:
- 60 official pi-mono example extensions (gold standard): hello, pirate, ssh, tools, plan-mode, doom-overlay, etc.
- 58 community extensions: mitsuhiko(10), hjanuschka(14), tmustier(12), nicobailon(6), qualisero(7), prateekmedia(6), others(3)
- 63 npm registry extensions: aliou-pi-*, pi-annotate, pi-brave-search, pi-mermaid, vaayne-*, walterra-*, etc.
- 23 third-party GitHub repos: aliou-pi-extensions(59 sub-exts), w-winter-dot314(40 sub-exts), etc.
- 7 agent collection directories

Multi-extension collections (aliou=59, w-winter=40) inflate individual entry count to 280+ extensions. After validation, expect 200-300 genuine loadable extensions.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:43Z"},{"id":3487,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE NOTES:

TS RUNTIME (pi-mono):
- loader.ts uses jiti (JIT TS loader by unjs)
- Extension factory: export default function(pi: ExtensionAPI) { ... }
- Registration methods write to Extension object: handlers, tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, messageRenderers
- Action methods delegate to shared ExtensionRuntime: sendMessage, exec, setModel, etc.
- Action methods throw during load (by design -- only registration happens at load time)
- Bun 1.3.8 available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun
- pi-mono source at legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/

RUST RUNTIME:
- extensions_js.rs: JsExtensionLoadSpec, swc TS compilation, QuickJS execution
- extensions.rs: CompatibilityScanner (line 127-414), ExtensionManager, dispatch
- JsExtensionSnapshot captures: tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers
- Hostcall bridge: JS pi.events(op,payload) -> Rust dispatch_hostcall_events()
- Session bridge: JS pi.session(op,payload) -> Rust dispatch_hostcall_session()
- ExtensionSession trait: get_state, get_messages, set_name, set_model, set_label

SHARED CONCERNS:
- Both runtimes must use identical mock specs for deterministic comparison
- Timestamps, random values, file paths must all be deterministic
- Event payloads must be identical in shape and content","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:45Z"},{"id":3488,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Epic complete: All 9 phases closed. VALIDATED_MANIFEST covers 210 extensions across 5 source tiers. Differential TS↔Rust oracle runs in CI (fast/full/weekly). Conformance report generator produces 3 output formats. 100% pass rate on official 60, 81.9% overall across all tiers. Phase 8 CI integration fully wired.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:33Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3oib1","title":"Interactive tree/branch overlays close on failed navigation start","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:59:15.173157238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:59:24.886917582Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3ok7w","title":"[AUTH-5] /login provider listing: show available OAuth providers and status","description":"## Problem

/login with no provider argument defaults to the current provider. Users cannot discover which providers support OAuth.

## Solution

### /login with No Args Shows Provider Table
```
Available OAuth providers:
  anthropic  ✅ Authenticated (expires in 23 hours)
  openai     ❌ Not authenticated
  google     ❌ Not authenticated

Extension providers:
  my-custom  ✅ Authenticated

Usage: /login <provider>
```

### Implementation
- Enumerate built-in OAuth providers (anthropic, openai, google)
- Check auth.json for credentials for each
- For OAuth credentials, show expiry status
- Enumerate extension-registered providers with OAuth configs
- Show table using tui.rs Table rendering

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: /login handler (no-arg case)
- src/auth.rs: Add method to list credential status per provider

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /login with no args shows provider table
- [ ] Shows authentication status for each provider
- [ ] Shows expiry time for OAuth tokens
- [ ] Includes extension-registered providers
- [ ] Unit test for provider listing","notes":"Implemented /login no-arg provider status listing with built-in + extension providers, added auth credential status API with alias fallback, and added unit tests for listing + status semantics. Quality gates: focused tests pass; cargo check --all-targets pass; cargo fmt --check pass; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:39.918601708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:23:37.960591181Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:23:37.960565663Z","close_reason":"Completed AUTH-5 acceptance criteria and quality gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ok7w","depends_on_id":"bd-2v8ux","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ok7w","depends_on_id":"bd-lufy2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ol8","title":"Trace core extension-unit modules in traceability matrix","description":"Add high-signal untraced extension runtime/conformance unit tests to docs/traceability_matrix.json under the comprehensive verification requirement, then re-run governance and staleness checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T09:56:39.507922384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T09:58:43.125868981Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T09:58:43.125846429Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3on","title":"Implement legacy capture pipeline (run + record outputs)","description":"Background:
- We need golden fixtures from legacy pi for each scenario.

Steps:
- Build a capture runner that executes scenarios and records raw outputs (stdout/stderr, JSON results).
- Record metadata: pi-mono commit, extension version, environment snapshot.
- Ensure the runner can be re-run deterministically.
- Emit structured capture logs per the logging spec for traceability.

Acceptance:
- For a small pilot subset, the capture output is repeatable and complete.
- Logs enable debugging without rerunning capture.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:37.848853767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:26:46.823520219Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T09:36:16.926205121Z","close_reason":"Added pi_legacy_capture runner (print/json + mock OpenAI) capturing pilot scenarios scn-permission-gate-001 + scn-plan-mode-002 deterministically","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-2qd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-7l6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-u4r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ooc8","title":"Preserve explicit env tombstones in VCR test overrides","description":"Fresh-eyes audit of the Copilot/VCR workflow found a determinism bug in src/vcr.rs test helpers. set_test_env_var() returns Option<String>, which cannot distinguish an absent override from an explicit tombstone (None meaning intentionally unset). After a nested override, restore_test_env_var() can remove the tombstone and fall back to the real process environment. Use a tri-state snapshot type and add a focused regression.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:44:04.160078004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:51:04.770601891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T01:51:04.770575562Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3oowx","title":"PARITY-CLI: CLI Flag Parity — Extension flag pass-through and env var support","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:43:15.225345519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:03:46.174670091Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T00:03:46.174644623Z","close_reason":"All dependency beads closed (CLI.1, V2 parity validation, env var support); epic acceptance satisfied","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oowx","depends_on_id":"bd-2cd9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oowx","depends_on_id":"bd-31ylu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oowx","depends_on_id":"bd-hxbav","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3829,"issue_id":"bd-3oowx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alignment note: linked to DROPIN-140 epic so CLI parity closure is managed by one canonical compatibility backlog.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:52:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3opk","title":"Gap: QuickJS resolver lacks native npm module support (node-pty, chokidar, jsdom, etc.)","description":"# Problem
8 extensions from seed-42 random trials fail because they import npm packages that require native Node.js bindings or complex dependency trees not available in the QuickJS sandbox.

# Failing Extensions
| Extension | Missing Module |
|---|---|
| npm/pi-mermaid | beautiful-mermaid |
| npm/pi-interactive-shell | node-pty |
| npm/aliou-pi-guardrails | @aliou/pi-utils-settings |
| npm/pi-brave-search | @mozilla/readability |
| community/nicobailon-interactive-shell | node-pty |
| third-party/marckrenn-pi-sub | @marckrenn/pi-sub-shared |
| npm/pi-watch | chokidar |
| npm/vaayne-pi-web-tools | jsdom |

# Analysis
These fall into two sub-categories:
1. **Native bindings** (node-pty): Cannot run in QuickJS at all — need a stub or "not supported" error
2. **Complex JS deps** (chokidar, jsdom, @mozilla/readability, beautiful-mermaid): Pure JS but huge dependency trees

# Possible approaches
- Virtual module stubs that throw helpful "not available in sandbox" errors
- For pure-JS deps: bundle with esbuild into the extension at install time
- Accept these as permanent N/A (native binding requirement)

# Evidence
- `tests/ext_conformance/reports/random_trials/RANDOM_TRIALS_REPORT.md`
- `target/ext_conformance/logs/random_trials/trial_seed_42.jsonl`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ScarletGate","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:51:13.951266725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:39:21.765490114Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:39:21.765396830Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2941,"issue_id":"bd-3opk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resetting to open — stalled with no progress from previous assignee. Available for pickup.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:57:28Z"},{"id":2942,"issue_id":"bd-3opk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ScarletGate: Claiming this bead. Will add virtual module stubs for npm packages that can't run in QuickJS sandbox, targeting the 8 resolver failures from seed-42 random trials.","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:16:29Z"},{"id":2943,"issue_id":"bd-3opk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ScarletGate: Completed. Added 16 virtual module stubs to QuickJS resolver.

## New Virtual Modules Added
1. **node-pty** - PTY spawn with mock process
2. **chokidar** - File watcher with chainable no-op watcher
3. **jsdom** - Minimal DOM (Element, JSDOM, querySelectorAll, textContent)
4. **@mozilla/readability** - Article parser returning safe defaults
5. **beautiful-mermaid** - Mermaid-to-ASCII placeholder
6. **@aliou/pi-utils-settings** - ConfigLoader, ArrayEditor, get/setNestedValue
7. **@aliou/sh** - Shell parser stubs (parse, tokenize, quote)
8. **@marckrenn/pi-sub-shared** - PROVIDERS, MODEL_MULTIPLIERS, getDefaultCoreSettings
9. **turndown** - HTML-to-Markdown converter (strip tags)
10. **turndown-plugin-gfm** - GFM plugin hooks
11. **@xterm/headless** - Terminal emulator stub
12. **@xterm/addon-serialize** - Serialization addon
13. **@opentelemetry/api** - Trace, context, SpanStatusCode
14. **@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http** - OTLP exporter
15. **@opentelemetry/resources** - Resource class
16. **@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-base** - BasicTracerProvider, BatchSpanProcessor
17. **@opentelemetry/semantic-conventions** - SemanticResourceAttributes
18. **@juanibiapina/pi-extension-settings** - getSetting with defaults

Also enhanced **node:util** shim: added stripVTControlCharacters, deprecate, inherits, debuglog, format, types.

## Impact
Random trials seed=42 n=50: **33/50 (66%) → 42/50 (84%)**
- Resolver failures: 8 → 1 (remaining: @sourcegraph/scip-typescript deep path)
- 9 more extensions now load and pass conformance

## Tests
- tests/npm_module_stubs.rs: 38 new tests (93 total with common module)
- All official conformance tests still pass
- Clippy clean","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:39:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oq","title":"Workstream: legacy pi reference capture complete","description":"Purpose:
- Run the *legacy* pi agent extension system and capture outputs for each sampled extension to create golden fixtures.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Deterministic capture runner and environment settings.
- Golden JSON fixtures per extension (tools, slash commands, events).
- Capture log + provenance (pi-mono commit, dependency versions, timestamps).

Definition of done:
- Every sampled extension has reference outputs captured for its supported behaviors.
- Fixtures are normalized and reproducible.
- Capture methodology documented so it can be repeated later.

Dependencies:
- Requires finalized sample list + archived artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:19:54.749923176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:29:43.384680427Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:09:29.593080231Z","close_reason":"All dependent capture tasks closed; fixtures+runner+docs exist; sample/artifact/fixture ids align","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-16n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-29c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-2qd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-3on","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-7l6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-u4r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oq","depends_on_id":"bd-vbs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:13Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3ors6","title":"Reject malformed Content-Length in HTTP client","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:40.663593967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:48.860126313Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3p29k","title":"DROPIN-172: Build comprehensive JSON mode unit test suite with detailed logs","description":"Add exhaustive unit tests for JSON mode event framing/schema/order, command queue state transitions, abort/interrupt races, and extension/tool event edge cases with rich structured logs.","design":"Implement deep unit tests for JSON mode parser/event contracts, state machine transitions, queue semantics, abort races, and extension/tool event boundaries with trace-rich logs.","acceptance_criteria":"Unit suite covers nominal/edge/failure paths and emits structured logs with scenario IDs, event traces, expected-vs-actual diffs, and timing metadata.","notes":"Complements fixture-level conformance by proving low-level correctness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyRidge","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:48.415092837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T02:44:23.180756814Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T02:44:23.180665333Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dropin","json-mode","logging","parity","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p29k","depends_on_id":"bd-2m6qw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p29k","depends_on_id":"bd-2my4b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p29k","depends_on_id":"bd-359pl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p29k","depends_on_id":"bd-37u8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p29k","depends_on_id":"bd-38v5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2495,"issue_id":"bd-3p29k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Added 34 new comprehensive tests (34-67) to json_mode_parity.rs covering:
- Event framing edge cases: empty text content, Unicode (emoji/CJK/RTL/zero-width), 1MB+ payload preservation
- Tool output: multiple content blocks, empty content array, various detail shapes (8 cases)
- Auto-compaction lifecycle matrix: 6 combinations of result/aborted/willRetry/errorMessage
- Auto-retry exponential backoff: delay doubling pattern validation, max_retries
- Extension error: all 12 hook names, with/without extensionId
- AME streaming sub-lifecycles: text (start→delta→delta→end), thinking, toolcall with ToolCall details
- AME stop reasons: all 5 StopReason variants in Done/Error
- skip_serializing_if correctness: 5 optional fields verified absent when None
- ContentBlock type tags: text/thinking/image/toolCall
- Mixed content blocks in AssistantMessage (thinking+text+toolCall)
- Full lifecycle with tool turn: 14-event sequence with ordering invariants
- Lifecycle with auto-retry mid-stream
- Lifecycle with auto-compaction between turns
- Turn index monotonic increase (5 turns)
- NDJSON single-line: multiline text, tabs, carriage returns, stack traces
- TurnEnd multiple tool results, AgentEnd message ordering
- Extension event payload roundtrip for all 10 forwarded events
- SessionHeader defaults and optional field absence
- Usage camelCase fields, multi-tool turn toolCallId uniqueness
- Content type camelCase: ThinkingContent, ImageContent, ToolCall
- Special characters in tool args: quotes, backslash, null bytes, control chars
- Extension UI: null payload fields, array payload, numeric payload

All 186 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:44:14Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ah1","title":"Implement session persistence cycle tests","description":"# Implement session persistence cycle tests

## Goal
Test the full session lifecycle: create → save → reload → verify integrity, with detailed logs.

## Background
Session persistence involves:
- JSONL file format (one JSON object per line)
- Header with metadata (version, cwd, timestamp)
- Message entries with parent/child relationships
- Tree structure for conversation branching
- File locking for concurrent access

Current tests cover serialization but not full I/O cycles.

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- Use temp dirs and deterministic paths; avoid asserting exact timestamps.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log session path, header fields, message IDs, tree shape, and file sizes.
- On failure, dump the JSONL contents (redacted) and parsed header.

## Test Categories

### 1. Basic Persistence Cycle Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_session_save_and_reload() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project/path");
    session.add_user_message("Hello");
    session.add_assistant_message("Hi there!");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.messages().len(), 2);
}
```

### 2. Tree Structure Persistence Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_branching_persists() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project");
    let root_id = session.add_user_message("Root");
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch A");

    session.navigate_to(root_id.clone());
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch B");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    let children = loaded.children_of(&root_id);
    assert_eq!(children.len(), 2);
}
```

### 3. Header Metadata Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_header_metadata_preserved() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/my/project/path");
    session.set_provider("anthropic");
    session.set_model("claude-opus-4");
    session.set_thinking_level(ThinkingLevel::High);
    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.cwd(), "/my/project/path");
}
```

### 4. Entry Type Persistence Tests
- ModelChange entries
- ThinkingLevel entries
- Compaction entries
- Tool invocation entries (if serialized)

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Missing file
- Corrupted JSONL
- Partial corruption (valid header + bad line)

### 6. Concurrent Access Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_concurrent_writes_locked() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let session1 = Session::new("/project");
    session1.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle1 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 1");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle2 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 2");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let (r1, r2) = join(handle1, handle2);
    assert!(r1.unwrap().is_ok() || r2.unwrap().is_ok());
}
```

### 7. Directory Structure Tests
- Persist creates nested directories when needed

## Dependencies
None (uses existing session module)

## Files
- tests/session_persistence.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ session persistence tests
- [ ] Save/reload cycle tested
- [ ] Tree structure preserved
- [ ] All entry types tested
- [ ] Error handling tested
- [ ] Concurrent access tested
- [ ] Logs include paths + header fields
- [ ] All tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test

**7 new tests added to tests/session_conformance.rs:**

1. save_preserves_header_provider_and_model
   - Tests provider, model_id, and thinking_level header fields survive save/reload

2. save_preserves_header_cwd

5. save_preserves_unicode_message_content

6. save_preserves_emoji_in_assistant_response
   - Tests emoji preservation in assistant response content blocks

7. save_preserves_message_timestamps

**Pre-existing tests already covered:**

Total: 37 tests passing, comprehensive session persistence coverage.

 {"id":"bd-anewk","title":"[PERF-4] Frame budget enforcement with degraded rendering modes","description":"## Problem

When the system is under load, the TUI continues trying to render at full fidelity 60fps, which can make the terminal unresponsive. Need automatic degradation.

## Solution: Frame Budget State Machine

### CRITICAL DESIGN: view() is &self — Interior Mutability Required

The charmed_bubbletea Model trait defines `fn view(&self) -> String`. The FrameBudget state machine transitions happen based on frame timing measured INSIDE view(). Since view() only has &self, all mutable FrameBudget fields must use Cell<T>.

Architecture:
- **FrameBudget** (Cell<T> fields) — frame-level state, updated in view(&self)
- **cpu_pressure** (plain PiApp field) — system-level state, updated in update(&mut self) via tick

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]
enum RenderFidelity { Normal, Degraded, Emergency }

/// Frame-level budget tracking. Uses Cell<T> because state transitions
/// occur inside view(&self) after measuring frame render time.
/// All fields are Copy types, so Cell (not RefCell) suffices.
struct FrameBudget {
    fidelity: Cell<RenderFidelity>,
    consecutive_over: Cell<u8>,   // frames exceeding budget
    consecutive_under: Cell<u8>,  // frames under budget (for recovery)
}

impl FrameBudget {
    /// Called at END of view() with the frame's render time.
    /// Updates fidelity for the NEXT frame (one-frame delay is imperceptible).
    fn record_frame(&self, frame_time: Duration) {
        let budget_us = match self.fidelity.get() {
            RenderFidelity::Normal => 16_000,    // 60fps
            RenderFidelity::Degraded => 33_000,  // 30fps
            RenderFidelity::Emergency => 66_000, // 15fps
        };
        if frame_time.as_micros() as u64 > budget_us {
            self.consecutive_under.set(0);
            let over = self.consecutive_over.get() + 1;
            self.consecutive_over.set(over);
            if over >= 3 {
                self.consecutive_over.set(0);
                let new_fidelity = match self.fidelity.get() {
                    RenderFidelity::Normal => RenderFidelity::Degraded,
                    RenderFidelity::Degraded => RenderFidelity::Emergency,
                    RenderFidelity::Emergency => RenderFidelity::Emergency,
                };
                self.fidelity.set(new_fidelity);
            }
        } else {
            self.consecutive_over.set(0);
            let under = self.consecutive_under.get() + 1;
            self.consecutive_under.set(under);
            if under >= 10 {
                self.consecutive_under.set(0);
                // Recovery with hysteresis (25% margin)
                let recovered = match self.fidelity.get() {
                    RenderFidelity::Emergency => RenderFidelity::Degraded,
                    RenderFidelity::Degraded => RenderFidelity::Normal,
                    RenderFidelity::Normal => RenderFidelity::Normal,
                };
                self.fidelity.set(recovered);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

Note: budget_us is DERIVED from fidelity, not a stored field. Fewer fields = fewer Cell operations = clearer code.

### CPU Pressure: Separate from FrameBudget

CPU pressure is a system-wide signal that changes slowly (load averages are 1-minute exponential moving averages). It is sampled in update(&mut self) via a periodic tick, NOT inside view().

```rust
// In PiApp (plain fields, updated in update(&mut self)):
cpu_pressure: PressureLevel,
cpu_pressure_last_sample: Instant,

// In update(&mut self, msg):
fn update(&mut self, msg: PiMsg) -> Cmd {
    // On every message, check if 5 seconds have elapsed
    if self.cpu_pressure_last_sample.elapsed() > Duration::from_secs(5) {
        self.cpu_pressure = sample_load_average(self.cpu_count);
        self.cpu_pressure_last_sample = Instant::now();
    }
    // ...
}

// In view(&self):
fn view(&self) -> String {
    // Combine frame budget with CPU pressure floor
    let frame_fidelity = self.frame_budget.fidelity.get();
    let effective = min(frame_fidelity, self.cpu_pressure.to_fidelity_floor());
    // Use effective fidelity for rendering decisions
    // ...
}
```

Platform-specific load average reading:
```rust
fn sample_load_average(cpu_count: usize) -> PressureLevel {
    let load = read_load_avg();  // returns Option<f64>
    match load {
        None => PressureLevel::Normal, // Non-Linux/macOS: always Normal
        Some(load) => {
            let ratio = load / cpu_count as f64;
            if ratio < 1.5 { PressureLevel::Normal }
            else if ratio < 3.0 { PressureLevel::Moderate }
            else { PressureLevel::High }
        }
    }
}

fn read_load_avg() -> Option<f64> {
    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    {
        let content = std::fs::read_to_string("/proc/loadavg").ok()?;
        content.split_whitespace().next()?.parse().ok()
    }
    #[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "freebsd"))]
    {
        let mut loadavg = [0.0f64; 1];
        let ret = unsafe { libc::getloadavg(loadavg.as_mut_ptr(), 1) };
        if ret == 1 { Some(loadavg[0]) } else { None }
    }
    #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "linux", target_os = "macos", target_os = "freebsd")))]
    { None }
}
```

### State Machine

```
Normal (60fps, full markdown)
  | 3 consecutive frames > 16ms
  v
Degraded (30fps, simplified markdown)
  | 3 consecutive frames > 33ms
  v
Emergency (15fps, raw text, no markdown)
  ^ 10 consecutive frames < budget (with 25% hysteresis margin)
```

### Degradation Levels

1. **Normal**: Full markdown rendering via glamour::render_with_word_wrap(), 60fps, all visual features
2. **Degraded**: Lightweight markdown (textwrap::fill() with bold/italic only, no syntax highlighting), effective 30fps via frame skipping (skip even-numbered frames, return cached view output)
3. **Emergency**: Raw text (no markdown at all), effective 15fps (skip 3 of 4 frames). Skip thinking blocks, minimal chrome.

### Frame Skipping Implementation
```rust
// In view(&self):
let frame_count = self.frame_timing.total_frames.get();
let fidelity = effective_fidelity;
let should_skip = match fidelity {
    RenderFidelity::Normal => false,
    RenderFidelity::Degraded => frame_count % 2 != 0,
    RenderFidelity::Emergency => frame_count % 4 != 0,
};
if should_skip {
    // Return cached last output (stored in RefCell<String>)
    return self.cached_view_output.borrow().clone();
}
```

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: FrameBudget struct (Cell<T> fields), cpu_pressure PiApp fields, integration into view()/update()

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-3 (frame timing telemetry -- need Instant::now() and total_frames counter)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FrameBudget with Cell<RenderFidelity>, Cell<u8> for counters
- [ ] cpu_pressure as plain PiApp field, sampled in update() via elapsed check
- [ ] Effective fidelity = min(frame_budget, cpu_pressure_floor)
- [ ] 3 consecutive over-budget frames trigger degradation
- [ ] 10 consecutive under-budget frames trigger recovery (with 25% hysteresis)
- [ ] Degraded mode: textwrap-only markdown, frame skipping (effective 30fps)
- [ ] Emergency mode: raw text, frame skipping (effective 15fps)
- [ ] Platform-specific load average: /proc/loadavg (Linux), libc::getloadavg (macOS), no-op (other)
- [ ] cached_view_output in RefCell<String> for frame skipping

## Unit Tests (in tests/tui_state.rs)
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_normal_to_degraded` -- 3 slow frames triggers Degraded
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_degraded_to_emergency` -- sustained slow triggers Emergency
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_recovery` -- 10 fast frames triggers recovery to Normal
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_hysteresis` -- borderline frames dont oscillate
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_cell_works_in_ref` -- verify Cell<T> works through &self reference
- `tui_perf_cpu_pressure_level_calculation` -- load/cpu_count mapped correctly
- `tui_perf_effective_fidelity_combines_budget_and_pressure` -- min() logic correct
- `tui_perf_frame_skip_degraded` -- even frames skipped in Degraded mode
- `tui_perf_frame_skip_emergency` -- 3 of 4 frames skipped in Emergency mode","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:07.564506084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:28.254003542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:28.253974759Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: FrameBudget state machine with Cell<T>, CPU pressure sampling from /proc/loadavg, effective fidelity combination, 3-frame degradation threshold, 10-frame recovery, Degraded (textwrap+30fps) and Emergency (raw+15fps) modes, cached_view_output for frame skipping. 9 unit tests passing. cargo check/clippy/fmt all clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-anewk","depends_on_id":"bd-b36e3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-axuu","title":"Workstream: /settings UI Parity + config persistence","description":"# Goal
Bring `/settings` and config editing UX to parity with legacy pi-mono:
- `/settings` opens an in-app UI for common options.
- Changes persist to JSON settings files with correct precedence (global vs project).

# Legacy Spec
- Settings docs: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md`
- `/settings` is listed in interactive command table in legacy README.

Legacy `/settings` covers (at least):
- thinking level
- theme
- message delivery (steering/follow-up)
- model cycling scope

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `/settings` prints a JSON summary only; no editor UI.
- Several config fields exist in `src/config.rs` but are not wired into the interactive UX.

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Implement a `/settings` UI component:
   - select/toggle settings
   - show current value
   - confirm/apply
2) Persist changes:
   - write to `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` (global) and/or `.pi/settings.json` (project)
   - follow existing merge semantics from `src/config.rs`
3) Ensure changes take effect without restart where possible.

# Notes
- Avoid editing raw JSON in the TUI for now; prefer focused selectors.
- For settings that depend on other workstreams (themes, keybindings), include partial functionality but keep UX consistent.

# Testing
- Unit tests for persistence and merge semantics.
- State tests for `/settings` selection flows.

# Dependencies
- Keybinding workstream (`bd-3ip`) for consistent navigation.
- Theme system workstream (`bd-22p`) for actual theme switching.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] /settings opens interactive UI and restores editor
[ ] Changes persist to global/project settings
[ ] Message delivery settings configurable
[ ] Theme selection supported (once theme system exists)
[ ] Display/editor toggles configurable
